steps.title: Recall the cylinder volume formula
steps.content: The volume of a right circular cylinder is given by the product of its base area and height. From the problem: radius $r = 12\text{ m}$ and height $h = 8\text{ m}$.

steps.title: Substitute the values and calculate
steps.content:
Substitute $r = 12$ and $h = 8$ into the formula:

$$V = \pi(12)^2(8) = \pi(144)(8) = 1152\pi\text{ m}^3$$

That matches choice D — D is correct.
